data.title: MemeCore (M) is now available on Binance Alpha
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Binance Alpha has launched MemeCore (M). Eligible users can use Binance Alpha points to claim 1,000 M tokens airdrop after the launch. This airdrop will be distributed in two phases: Phase 1 (first 18 hours): users with at least 214 Alpha points can claim; Phase 2 (last 6 hours): users with at least 140 Alpha points can participate, first come first served, until the airdrop pool is full or the event ends. Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Binance Alpha points.

data.title: Evolution of Money: From Shiny Coins to Smart Contracts
data.description:
What do seashells, gold coins, paper bills, and Bitcoin all have in common? They’re all part of the wild, weird, and surprisingly creative evolution of money —a story that stretches from bartering goats to scanning QR codes in coffee shops. Money isn’t some fixed, one-size-fits-all invention. It’s a tool we keep upgrading as our world—and our tech—changes. And it has changed
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/07/03/evolution-of-money-from-shiny-coins-to-smart-contracts/

data.title: AT&T Wireless Account Lets You Lock Your Phone Like a Vault
data.description:
AT&T Inc. , one of the largest telecommunications providers in the U. S. , has rolled out a new security feature called Wireless Account Lock, giving customers the ability to protect their accounts from SIM swapping attacks
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/07/03/att-wireless-account-lets-you-lock-your-phone-like-a-vault/

data.title: Quantum-Resistant Encryption Lands in Apple and Microsoft Updates
data.description:
Apple Inc. , the American tech giant, has announced that its upcoming operating system updates for Mac will include support for quantum-secure key exchange algorithms, enabling enhanced encryption when connecting to TLS 1. 3 servers that offer the same level of security. The announcement was made during Apple’s recent Worldwide Developers Conference, where the company outlined plans to integrate quantum-resistant encryption into its upcoming software
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/07/03/quantum-resistant-encryption-lands-in-apple-and-microsoft-updates/

data.title: A whale bought $3.645 million worth of ETH an hour ago
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to @ai_9684xtpa monitoring, a whale bought 1,406 ETH at an average price of $2,592.47 on the chain an hour ago, with a transaction amount of $3.645 million. The address has conducted three swing operations in the past month, with a current record of 2 wins and 1 loss, and a cumulative loss of $198,000.

data.title: UXLINK Increases Holdings in Bitcoin and UXLINK Tokens
data.description: According to Foresight News, Web3 social platform and infrastructure provider UXLINK announced via Twitter that it has increased its holdings by 1.37 BTC and 468,000 UXLINK tokens, valued at approximately $696,000. The company also stated its intention to continue allocating 40% of its monthly profits towards purchasing BTC and UXLINK tokens.

data.title: Nigerian Scammer Impersonates Trump Inaugural Committee Chair to Defraud Donor
data.description: According to BlockBeats, a Nigerian scammer impersonated Steve Witkoff, co-chair of the Trump Inaugural Committee, to defraud a donor of 250,000 USDT. The U.S. Department of Justice reported that the scammer used an email address with only slight character differences to deceive the victim. The FBI managed to trace approximately $40,000 through blockchain technology, with Tether assisting in freezing the related funds.

data.title: Ethereum Community Foundation Aims to Boost Ether Price to $10,000
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Ethereum core developer Zak Cole has established the Ethereum Community Foundation (ECF) with the goal of elevating the price of Ether (ETH) to $10,000. Cole introduced the ECF during the eighth Ethereum Community Conference in Cannes, France, emphasizing the need to address the Ethereum Foundation's perceived neglect of ETH as an asset class. The ECF plans to fund infrastructure projects that enhance the Ethereum ecosystem while simultaneously aiming to increase ETH's market value.

Cole stated that the ECF's mission is to serve ETH holders by taking actions that the Ethereum Foundation cannot or will not undertake. He highlighted the importance of ETH's price as a "direct function of security," advocating for its rise to the $10,000 mark. The ECF intends to provide grants to projects that contribute to ETH burn, public goods, core infrastructure advancement, and Ethereum scaling. These projects must utilize Ethereum as the settlement layer, leading to ETH burns through transaction processing on the Ethereum mainnet. Furthermore, the projects are required to be tokenless and immutable.

During his presentation, Cole criticized Uniswap, Ethereum Name Service, and Optimism for receiving support from the Ethereum Foundation and subsequently launching their own tokens backed by venture capital funding. He argued that publicly funded projects should remain public and benefit those who have financed them. Cole also expressed dissatisfaction with the Ethereum Foundation's approach to ETH as a valuable asset. Cointelegraph attempted to contact the Ethereum Foundation for comment but did not receive a response by the time of publication.

The Ethereum Validator Association (EVA) has been announced as the first recipient of a grant from the ECF. Cole explained that the EVA will empower validators by giving them a voice in shaping Ethereum's monetary policy, prioritizing EIP road maps, and selecting client priorities. He noted that the EVA introduces validator representation, allowing clients to be empowered based on their performance. Additionally, Cole pointed out a potential conflict of interest involving Offchain Labs, the team behind the layer-2 network Arbitrum, which holds an ownership stake in Prysmatic Labs. Prysmatic Labs operates the Prysm client, the second-largest consensus layer client of the Ethereum network, according to Ethernodes.

data.title: Coinsilium Group Subsidiary Forza Raises $3.8 Million for Bitcoin Investment
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Coinsilium Group's subsidiary, Forza, has successfully raised approximately $3. 8 million through the issuance of 15,486,111 new ordinary shares, each with a nominal value of zero. The shares were priced at £0. 18 each. The funds will be utilized to increase the company's holdings in Bitcoin.
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/26435593318001?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS
